2603.19101.title: FedTrident: Resilient Road Condition Classification Against Poisoning Attacks in Federated Learning
2603.19101.paper_summary: FL has emerged as a transformative paradigm for ITS, notably camera-based Road Condition Classification (RCC). However, by enabling collaboration, FL-based RCC exposes the system to adversarial participants launching Targeted Label-Flipping Attacks (TLFAs). Malicious clients (vehicles) can relabel their local training data (e.g., from an actual uneven road to a wrong smooth road), consequently compromising global model predictions and jeopardizing transportation safety. Existing countermeasures against such poisoning attacks fail to maintain resilient model performance near the necessary attack-free levels in various attack scenarios due to: 1) not tailoring poisoned local model detection to TLFAs, 2) not excluding malicious vehicular clients based on historical behavior, and 3) not remedying the already-corrupted global model after exclusion. To close this research gap, we propose FedTrident, which introduces: 1) neuron-wise analysis for local model misbehavior detection (notably including attack goal identification, critical feature extraction, and GMM-based model clustering and filtering); 2) adaptive client rating for client exclusion according to the local model detection results in each FL round; and 3) machine unlearning for corrupted global model remediation once malicious clients are excluded during FL. Extensive evaluation across diverse FL-RCC models, tasks, and configurations demonstrates that FedTrident can effectively thwart TLFAs, achieving performance comparable to that in attack-free scenarios and outperforming eight baseline countermeasures by 9.49% and 4.47% for the two most critical metrics. Moreover, FedTrident is resilient to various malicious client rates, data heterogeneity levels, complicated multi-task, and dynamic attacks.
2603.19101.paper_summary_zh: FL 已經成為智慧交通系統（ITS）的一個變革性範式，特別是在基於攝影機的道路狀況分類（RCC）方面。<lb/>然而，通過促進合作，基於 FL 的 RCC 使系統暴露於發起針對性標籤翻轉攻擊（TLFAs）的對抗性參與者。<lb/>惡意客戶端（車輛）可以重新標記其本地訓練數據（例如，將實際不平坦的道路標記為錯誤的平坦道路），從而損害全球模型的預測並危及交通安全。<lb/>現有針對此類污染攻擊的對策未能在各種攻擊場景中維持接近必要的無攻擊水平的穩健模型性能，原因包括：1）未能針對 TLFAs 定制污染的本地模型檢測，2）未能根據歷史行為排除惡意車輛客戶端，以及 3）未能在排除後修復已經損壞的全球模型。<lb/>為了填補這一研究空白，我們提出了 FedTrident，該方法引入了：1）針對本地模型不當行為檢測的神經元級分析（特別包括攻擊目標識別、關鍵特徵提取以及基於 GMM 的模型聚類和過濾）；<lb/>2）根據每個 FL 回合中的本地模型檢測結果進行的自適應客戶端評級，以便排除客戶端；以及 3）在 FL 中排除惡意客戶端後，對損壞的全球模型進行機器遺忘修復。<lb/>在各種 FL-RCC 模型、任務和配置中進行的廣泛評估顯示，FedTrident 能夠有效阻止 TLFAs，實現與無攻擊場景中相當的性能，並在兩個最關鍵的指標上超越八個基準對策，分別達到 9.49% 和 4.47% 的提升。此外，FedTrident 對各種惡意客戶端比率、數據異質性水平、複雜的多任務和動態攻擊具有穩健性。
2603.19101.author: Sheng Liu et.al.
